title: rhododendron nudipes var. lagopus
intro:
Rhododendron nudipes var. lagopus, also known as the Tibetan rhododendron or rhododendron with bare feet due to the hairy, scale-like leaves resembling bare feet, is a plant species that belongs to the heath family and is native to the Sichuan and Yunnan provinces of China.

The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. It has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and rheumatism, offering relief from pain and inflammation. The plant can be used to prepare herbal tea, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine in the form of dried roots and rhizomes.
